title: Cat Yellow Eye Discharge: Causes, Urgency, and Care Guide
meta_desc: Noticed yellow discharge from your cat's eyes? Learn what causes it, why it is medium urgency, and how to help your feline friend today.
content_html:
What is Yellow Eye Discharge?

Yellow or greenish discharge from a cat's eyes is typically a sign of an infection or inflammation. While clear tearing can sometimes be caused by allergies or dust, thick colored discharge usually indicates that the immune system is responding to a bacterial or viral pathogen, such as Feline Herpesvirus or Calicivirus. It may also be related to conjunctivitis or an underlying respiratory issue.

Is It an Emergency?

The urgency level for yellow discharge from both eyes is Medium. This is generally not a life-threatening emergency unless your cat is having difficulty breathing or is completely lethargic. However, eye issues can progress rapidly and become painful. You should aim to schedule a veterinary exam within 24 to 48 hours to prevent potential damage to the corneas or chronic respiratory complications.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Taking a clear, well-lit photo of your cat's eyes can be an invaluable tool for your veterinarian or a triage specialist. A photo allows a professional to see the exact color and thickness of the discharge, look for signs of swelling (chemosis), and determine if the third eyelid is elevated. This visual information helps determine if your cat needs an immediate urgent care visit or if a standard appointment will suffice.

faqs.question: Can I use human eye drops for my cat?
faqs.answer: No. Never use human over-the-counter eye drops on a cat, as some ingredients can be toxic or cause severe irritation. Only use medications prescribed by a veterinarian.

faqs.question: How can I make my cat more comfortable while waiting for the vet?
faqs.answer: You can gently wipe away the crust and discharge using a soft cloth or cotton ball dampened with lukewarm water. Use a fresh surface for each eye to avoid spreading bacteria.

faqs.question: Is this condition contagious to other cats?
faqs.answer: Yes, many causes of yellow eye discharge, such as feline respiratory infections, are highly contagious. It is best to keep the affected cat separated from other pets until they have been evaluated.
